_meta.purpose: Extraction inventory for the RAC (Requirements as Code) specification. Every normative claim in SPEC.md cites a key in this file via an HTML comment (<!-- inv: <key> -->). This is a working artifact of the spec extraction; it is not itself normative.
_meta.source_of_truth: src/rac/ validator code: core/validation.py, core/classification.py, core/artifacts.py, core/frontmatter.py, core/metadata.py, core/identity.py, core/idgen.py, core/relationship_types.py, core/markdown.py, core/corpus.py, core/fs.py, core/okf.py, core/overrides.py, core/limits.py, services/validate.py, services/relationships.py, services/review.py, services/gate.py, services/okf_conformance.py, services/init.py, output/sarif.py, cli.py
_meta.verified_by_running: rac validate rac/, rac relationships rac/ --validate, rac gate rac/ against the rac-core dogfood corpus (389 artifacts, all pass)

artifact_types.source: core/artifacts.py ARTIFACT_SPECS; frontmatter `type` is validated against this registry (core/frontmatter.py spec_for check)
artifact_types.unknown_is_valid: A document matching no type classifies as 'unknown'. Unknown is a valid, successful outcome, not an error (core/classification.py). Directory validation reports unknown files as skipped, never failed (services/validate.py STATUS_SKIPPED).

classification.mechanism: Pure section-presence scoring: points = |matched required| + 0.5 * |matched recommended|; fit = points / ceiling. Best fit wins; ties broken by more required matches then registry order.
classification.unknown_condition: fit < 0.5 OR zero required sections matched
classification.synonyms: Per-type heading synonyms applied before matching (e.g. 'success criteria' -> 'success metrics'); classification aid only — validation expects canonical headings
classification.separation: Classification is separate from validation: an invalid but recognizable artifact still classifies as its type, then fails validation

file_format.carrier: One artifact per UTF-8 Markdown (.md) file, optional leading YAML frontmatter block delimited by --- (closer may be --- or ...), body parsed as CommonMark
file_format.title: Exactly one top-level # heading (missing-title / multiple-titles are errors)
file_format.sections: ## headings, matched case-insensitively and whitespace-trimmed; section bodies are the unit of structural validation
file_format.discovery: Corpus walk = sorted *.md files under the corpus root, recursive, skipping any path component starting with '.' (core/fs.py find_markdown_files)

frontmatter.required: schema_version (integer) is required WHEN a frontmatter block is present; a document with no frontmatter at all is legal (legacy form)
frontmatter.id: optional string; must match ID grammar (invalid-id-syntax error otherwise); normalized to uppercase
frontmatter.type: optional string; must be a registered artifact type (invalid-metadata-field error otherwise)
frontmatter.relationships: optional mapping of relationship kind -> list of artifact-ID strings; shape-validated only; RESERVED — parsed but not consumed by relationship analysis (core/metadata.py docstring); kinds are NOT checked against the relationship registry
frontmatter.tags: optional list of non-empty strings (OKF-reserved descriptive field, ADR-050)
frontmatter.timestamps: Deliberately absent: recency is git-derived, never stored in frontmatter (ADR-045)
frontmatter.strictness: Malformed YAML, duplicate keys (duplicate-frontmatter-key), aliases (forbidden), nesting > 32 levels, and blocks > 64 KiB are all errors (malformed-frontmatter); unterminated opening --- is malformed-frontmatter

id_rules.grammar: ^[A-Z][A-Z0-9]{1,9}-[0-9A-HJKMNP-TV-Z]{12}$ — <REPOSITORY_KEY>-<12-char Crockford base32 suffix (no I/L/O/U)>; matched case-insensitively, normalized uppercase
id_rules.repository_key: ^[A-Z][A-Z0-9]{1,9}$ — declared once in .rac/config.yaml repository_key (rac init); changing an established key is an error, never a silent rewrite
id_rules.suffix: 8 chars millisecond timestamp (time-sortable, ULID-style) + 4 chars CSPRNG; generation is branch-safe and offline
identity_precedence: spec.id_field declared section (no type sets it today)
id_rules.aliases: An artifact answers to ALL of the above as resolution aliases (migration support); duplicate-identity detection uses ONLY the canonical identifier
id_rules.conflict: frontmatter id vs differing declared legacy identity = conflicting-identity (error); never silently resolved
id_rules.uniqueness_scope: Canonical identifier must be unique corpus-wide: duplicate-artifact-identifier (error, relationships source)
id_rules.path_independence: Identity is the explicit id, never the file path. Moving or renaming a file does not change a frontmatter-declared identity; inbound references keep resolving. NOTE: the extraction brief attributed this to the 2026.06.5 'rename' release; in fact 2026.06.5 renamed the PyPI package, and path-independent identity dates from ADR-026/v0.7.11 (opaque frontmatter IDs). Resolved at ratification: cite ADR-026, not the release.
id_rules.rename_refactor: rac rename computes a corpus-wide, deterministic, reversible edit plan for an id rename; token-specific, refuses filename-only identities (services/rename.py)
id_rules.requirement_line_ids: Inline [REQ-NNN] requirement-line IDs are NOT artifact identifiers; relationship targets are whole artifact files (core/identity.py)
id_rules.source: core/metadata.py ID_RE, core/idgen.py, core/identity.py, services/init.py KEY_RE, services/relationships.py

sections_per_type.notes: Required sections must be present (error when missing); recommended sections never fail (review priority-4 advisory when absent); optional sections are recognized/extracted but never scored, never reported missing. Missing-required code pattern: missing-<section-with-hyphens>.

status_enums.location: ## Status body section (first non-empty line), NOT frontmatter; matched case-insensitively; a missing Status section is always legal (metadata is optional, validated-if-present)

roadmap.note: Achieved is a live TERMINAL state (ADR-061): realized intent, still valid to reference, not retired

status_enums.unknown_value: invalid-<type>-status (error, blocking by default) — e.g. invalid-decision-status

roadmap.horizon.allowed: now | next | later | calendar quarter matching ^Q[1-4] \d{4}$

status_enums.transitions: NO state-machine transitions are enforced by the validator. Status is enum-membership-validated only. Lifecycle semantics are carried by the live/retired partition: a retired artifact must not be referenced by a live artifact except via supersedes (see relationship checks).
status_enums.supersession_semantics: When an artifact moves to a retired status: (1) every inbound reference from a LIVE artifact via any edge with forbids_target_status=true becomes relationship-target-superseded (warning severity, blocking by default in rac gate); (2) the supersedes edge is exempt (the replacing decision legitimately points at the one it retires); (3) references FROM retired artifacts are exempt (historical chains); (4) MCP/consumer surfaces flag retired artifacts (grounding eval treats a surfaced superseded decision as a hard negative).
status_enums.source: core/artifacts.py metadata/retired_status, core/validation.py _validate_status_metadata, services/relationships.py _is_retired_artifact + status-consistency pass

relationship_types.declaration: Relationships are explicit Markdown sections (## Related Decisions, ## Supersedes, ...) whose body lines are references — one reference per non-empty line, well-formed leading list marker stripped, otherwise line preserved verbatim (the line text IS the reference; ADR-016)

supersedes.note: the ONLY edge exempt from the retired-target rule

related_tickets.note: target is an external ticket key/URL; format-linted against .rac/config.yaml ticketing.provider (jira|github|linear|azure-devops|servicenow|none); never resolved in-corpus

verified_by.note: targets are external test/trace file paths; recorded, not resolved, not existence-checked

applies_to.note: targets are repo paths/globs/component labels a decision governs; LITERAL path entries (containing '/', no glob chars) are existence-checked against the repo root (applies-to-target-not-found, error); globs and component labels are recorded without checking

relationship_types.domain_enforcement: Edge legality by source type = the section appears in the type's spec.optional. A recognized vocabulary section populated on a type that does not declare it = relationship-edge-unsupported (warning severity, blocking by default in gate); it produces no edge
relationship_types.unrecognized_sections: A ## heading OUTSIDE the vocabulary (e.g. '## Related Widgets') is an ordinary body section: no edge, NO finding. Frontmatter relationships: kinds are shape-validated but not vocabulary-checked (reserved). Accepted as-is at ratification; the spec follows the code, and unknown kinds must become blocking when the field is activated.
relationship_types.referential_integrity: Every non-external reference is resolved against the corpus alias index: relationship-target-not-found (error) / relationship-target-ambiguous (error) / relationship-self-reference (warning); resolved targets are range-checked (relationship-target-type-mismatch, error; untyped targets exempt) and status-checked (relationship-target-superseded, warning). supersedes graphs must be acyclic (relationship-cycle, error, per strongly-connected component).
relationship_types.all_relationship_findings_fail_validate: rac relationships --validate exits 1 on ANY finding regardless of intrinsic severity; rac gate defaults every relationship finding to blocking

requirement_line_grammar.pattern: A requirement line under ## Requirements is [REQ-NNN] <description>: bracketed ID matching ^REQ-\d+$ followed by non-empty text
requirement_line_grammar.quality_rules: requirement-normative-keyword (error): lowercase/mixed-case shall|must|should is ambiguous — only ALL-CAPS BCP-14 keywords carry weight; requirement-not-singular (warning): >1 normative keyword per line (ISO 29148); requirement-non-ears (warning): no normative keyword; requirement-ears-clause (warning): opens with 'If' but no 'then'

enforcement_class.decides: the rac gate exit code: exit 0 iff zero blocking findings; the SARIF level carries intrinsic severity, the exit code carries enforcement

1.name: invalid artifact
1.meaning: validation errors: the artifact fails its schema

2.name: broken relationship
2.meaning: a declared reference does not resolve (unresolved, ambiguous, or self)

6.name: suspect drift
6.note: git-derived: referenced target changed after the referrer

severity_model.review_fail_rule: ReviewReport.ok is false iff any finding has priority <= 2 (PRIORITY_BROKEN_RELATIONSHIP). Priorities 3-6 are advisory and never fail.
severity_model.note_on_four_tiers: The extraction brief described 'four severity tiers'. The code defines SIX review priority levels; 5 and 6 are later advisory-only additions (v0.13.3, drift phase 1). Priorities 1-2 fail, 3-6 advisory — the '1-2 fail CI' claim holds. Accepted as-is at ratification: the spec specifies all six.
severity_model.policy: A corpus MAY reclassify per finding code via .rac/config.yaml enforcement: {blocking: [...], advisory: [...], off: [...]}; precedence off > blocking > advisory > default. Severity overrides (validation: rules/types) may downgrade error->warning or suppress (off) per rule code or cap a type at warning (ADR-053). Both live in the committed config so runs stay deterministic.

malformed-frontmatter.notes: bad YAML, not a mapping, aliases, depth > 32, block > 64 KiB, unterminated ---

invalid-metadata-field.notes: unknown frontmatter field, missing/mistyped schema_version, non-string id, unregistered type, malformed relationships/tags shape

missing-<required-section>.notes: per type; codes: missing-problem, missing-requirements, missing-context, missing-decision, missing-consequences, missing-outcomes, missing-initiatives, missing-objective, missing-input, missing-instructions, missing-output, missing-user-need, missing-design, missing-constraints

relationship-target-superseded.notes: warning-severity but blocking by default — the annotation level and the enforcement class are independent

gate_semantics.composition: rac gate = validate (incl. OKF conformance) + relationships --validate + review over ONE corpus walk, each finding mapped to (source, code, intrinsic severity, enforcement class), policy applied, findings sorted by (path, line, source, code, message)
gate_semantics.exit_contract: exit 0 iff zero blocking findings; advisory findings never fail the gate
gate_semantics.ci_wiring: pr-gate-action runs `rac gate <path> --sarif`, uploads one SARIF under Code Scanning category rac-gate, and fails the check iff the CLI exit is non-zero. The action never reinterprets findings (ADR-063). The pre-merge check tier is a required merge gate on main (ADR-075).
gate_semantics.sarif: SARIF 2.1.0; level from intrinsic severity (error/warning/note); enforcement class carried by the exit code, NOT the SARIF level; deterministic (sorted results, no timestamps)
gate_semantics.determinism: same corpus state => byte-identical report, JSON, and SARIF (ADR-002)

exit_codes.0: success (rac validate: no errors; relationships --validate: no findings; review: no priority 1-2; gate: no blocking findings)
exit_codes.1: findings: validate errors; relationships --validate any finding; review priority 1-2; gate blocking

spec_version_declaration.per_artifact: frontmatter schema_version (integer, required when frontmatter present, supported = {1}, unsupported-schema-version error on anything else) — a per-ARTIFACT envelope version
spec_version_declaration.corpus_level: NONE. The corpus root config .rac/config.yaml exists (keys read today: repository_key, ticketing.provider, validation.rules/types, enforcement.blocking/advisory/off, audit) but the engine reads no corpus-level spec-version key.
spec_version_declaration.spec_addition: SPEC.md section 10 specifies `rac_spec: "0.1"` in .rac/config.yaml — the one authorized forward-looking addition. rac-core does not read it yet; at ratification the maintainer committed rac-core to implementing it (read the key, refuse newer versions, declare it in the dogfood corpus) before the v0.1.0 announcement, so the MUST stands (SPEC.md section 10.1 status note).

reserved_structure.config: .rac/config.yaml at the corpus/repo root (discovered by upward walk from the working directory); holds repository_key (required for ID generation), ticketing.provider, validation severity overrides, enforcement policy
reserved_structure.okf_reserved_filenames: index.md and log.md are OKF-reserved generated entry points: a TYPED artifact at either name is okf-reserved-filename-collision (error); an untyped document there is legal
reserved_structure.discovery: *.md under the corpus root, sorted, recursive by default, dot-directories skipped; no other filenames or directory layout are reserved — directory conventions (rac/decisions/ etc.) are conventions, not normative

okf_composition.claim: A RAC corpus is expressible as a conformant OKF v0.1 bundle (ADR-048: every RAC repository MUST be a conformant OKF v0.1 bundle); rac export --okf emits the derived bundle: one file per typed artifact with projected frontmatter (type mapped per OKF_TYPE, id, git-derived created/updated, tags), resolved relationships rendered as # Citations body links, generated index.md + log.md
okf_composition.direction: one-way: RAC -> OKF always; OKF -> validated RAC is not generally possible (untyped links, open type set, no lifecycle)
okf_composition.okf_spec_verified: GoogleCloudPlatform/knowledge-catalog okf/SPEC.md fetched 2026-07-05: section 5.3 — 'The specific kind of relationship ... is conveyed by the surrounding prose, not by the link itself'; consumers 'MUST tolerate broken links'. Section 9 — consumers MUST NOT reject over missing optional fields, unknown type values, unknown keys, broken cross-links, missing index.md; consumers SHOULD preserve unknown keys (SHOULD, not MUST — SPEC.md section 5 deliberately says 'directed to preserve', not MUST). Section 11 — okf_version MAY be declared in bundle-root index.md frontmatter; consumers that do not understand it SHOULD attempt best-effort consumption. Required frontmatter: type only.

limits.note: robustness caps; oversize/truncation is reported as structured findings, never a crash

ticketing_providers.offline: pure format lint; the engine never contacts the ticketing system
